topic.what: TOCTOU vulnerability (Visual Atomicity Violation) in desktop GUI agents exploiting the observation-to-action gap
topic.how: Attacker injects UI state change during the 6.51s gap between screenshot capture and action execution; three primitives: notification overlay hijack, window focus manipulation, DOM injection
topic.why_matters: Unprivileged attackers can redirect agent actions (e.g., approve fraudulent transfers) without modifying agent code or LLM reasoning; fundamentally unaddressed by prompt-level defenses

characteristics.what_it_measures: Attack success rate: fraction of trials where click lands in attacker-controlled zone (Spatial-ASR)
characteristics.evidence: Table 3: 45/45 trials for Primitive B, 15/15 trials per task
characteristics.context: Primitive B (Window Focus Manipulation) achieves 100% Spatial-ASR and Trigger-ASR across all tasks

characteristics.what_it_measures: Temporal window between screenshot capture and action dispatch
characteristics.evidence: Table 1: n=10 tasks, σ=3.59s, min=3.18s, max=13.23s
characteristics.context: Sufficient for reliable exploitation; comparable to Android Action Rebinding (4.18-15.43s)

characteristics.what_it_measures: Action Interception Rate: fraction of attacks blocked by PUSV
characteristics.evidence: Tables 7-8: 100% AIR against Primitive A (135 trials) and Primitive B (45 trials)
characteristics.context: PUSV (Layer 1+2a+2b) achieves 100% interception for OS-level attacks; ≈0% for DOM injection (structural blind spot)

characteristics.what_itasures: Additional latency added by PUSV verification before each action
characteristics.context: Negligible relative to 6.51s mean gap; deterministic signals vs LLM-based re-check would add ~5s

characteristics.what_it_measures: PUSV abort rate on benign trials with no attacker present
characteristics.evidence: 30 benign trials, zero PUSV aborts; SSIM range 0.97-1.00, global diff <0.04%
characteristics.context: No false alarms on legitimate UI transitions (cursor blink, page scroll, animations)

characteristics.what_it_measures: PUSV interception rate against zero-visual-footprint DOM injection attacks
characteristics.evidence: Table 9: 0% L1-AIR, 0% L2b-AIR, 1/45 incidental L2a for Primitive C (45 trials)
characteristics.context: Structural blind spot of visual-based verification; transparent overlay produces pixel-identical screenshots

good_for: cross-application desktop agents using screenshot-and-click loops
detecting UI state changes between observation and action
not_for: Pure web-based agents without OS-level interaction
requires: Screenshot capture API at action dispatch time
wmctrl or equivalent window registry access
Calibration on ~30 benign trials per desktop environment

novelty: First formalization of Visual Atomicity Violation (VAV) for desktop GUI agents
DesktopTOCTOU-Bench: 50 adversarial scenarios across 5 domains (file ops, comms, config, data access, privilege escalation)
PUSV: lightweight three-layer defense achieving 100% AIR against OS-level attacks with <0.1s overhead
Demonstration that visual defenses have structural blind spot against zero-visual-footprint DOM injections
